title: Arrivals of tourists from abroad for personal purposes
caption:
Arrivals of tourists from abroad for personal purposes

Number of international trips where the main purpose is personal (leisure, visiting friends and relatives, health, etc.) and the visitor stays overnight.

This choropleth world map displays 2024 data on the number of international tourist arrivals for personal purposes. The legend ranges from 0 to over 10 million, with specific thresholds marked at 500,000, 1 million, 5 million, and 10 million. The darkest blue band, representing values above 10 million, is occupied by the United States, Mexico, Turkey, and Japan. Several countries sit in the next tier, falling between 5 million and 10 million; these include Canada, Spain, Italy, Saudi Arabia, India, and Australia. A broader group of nations appears in the mid-range blue shades, indicating arrival numbers between 1 million and 5 million. This cluster includes the United Kingdom, France, Germany, Brazil, Argentina, South Africa, and Indonesia. Lighter blue shades, corresponding to values between 500,000 and 1 million, are visible in countries such as Chile, Peru, and Egypt. Many countries across Africa, Central Asia, and parts of South America appear in the lightest shade or white, indicating values near zero or below the 500,000 threshold. Large areas, including Russia, China, Greenland, and much of the African continent, are covered in diagonal hatching, which the legend identifies as "No data." The visual distribution shows the highest volumes of personal travel concentrated in North America, parts of Southern Europe, the Middle East, and East Asia, while vast regions lack reported figures for this specific metric.
